---
title: "3 · Cyberinfrastructure"
---

The twin is only as good as the data plane that feeds it. The hard part is not any one stream — it is
that the streams arrive at wildly different native resolutions, cadences, and error models, and must be
reconciled *without* pretending a coarse product is a fine measurement. This chapter shows that plane
end to end: how each source is fetched, homogenised, staged, and handed to assimilation.

![The data plane. Sources at their native resolution are fetched by a stream-specific loader, reprojected
to the frozen 90 m grid, and — critically — kept at their own footprint through a forward operator $g_i$
rather than regridded. Everything is staged as Zarr in the earth2studio `DataSource` layout on Kopah
S3, then assimilated by the BLUE at each datum's native support. Evaluation streams enter the same way
but are held out to score the product, never to force it.](assets/cyberinfra.png)

## Fetch: one loader per stream, offline-safe

Each source has a dedicated loader under `src/data/` (`fetch_prism_daily`, `fetch_usgs_discharge`,
`fetch_insitu_sm`, `fetch_snotel_swe`, `fetch_gauge_basins`, `fetch_solus_domain`,
`download_3dep_domain`, `fetch_earth2studio`). Two rules are enforced across all of them:

- **Fail loud, never fabricate.** A loader that cannot reach its provider raises, or falls back to a
  cached table of the *same* network — it never substitutes a plausible-looking default. The FuXi
  forcing loader refuses a zero-precipitation field by name and by magnitude
  ([Appendix A](A-compute-and-deployment.qmd)), because an all-zero PNW wet-season forecast is a bug,
  not a drought.
- **Offline fallbacks.** Figures and analyses degrade to cached parquet rather than failing because a
  provider's DNS is down.

## Homogenise: the forward operator, not regridding

This is the step that makes cross-resolution assimilation well-posed. Every stream is reprojected to
the single frozen analysis grid (`src/config/domain.py`, EPSG:5070, 90 m), but the *observation* is
**not** resampled to 90 m. Instead each datum keeps its native footprint as a forward operator $g_i$ —
a point for a well or probe, a coda-kernel volume for dv/v, a top-hat pixel for a satellite, a basin
integral for a gauge. The model is then upscaled to the datum through $g_i$ and differenced there
("upscale-then-compare"). A 9 km retrieval constrains the 9 km average of the fine field and nothing
finer; footprint leakage is impossible by construction. The full estimator is in
[Assimilation](04-assimilation.qmd).

## Stage: Zarr on Kopah, in the earth2studio layout

Every homogenised stream is written as Zarr following the `gaia-cli` convention (obstore +
`zarr_format=3`, `consolidated=False`, ~10 MB chunks) to Kopah S3:

```
s3://gaia/soil-twin/static/     SVM / Vs30 / SOLUS / terrain
s3://gaia/soil-twin/forcing/    PRISM daily (observed)
s3://gaia/soil-twin/forecast/   FuXi initialisations (predicted)
s3://gaia/soil-twin/obs/        wells, probes, gauges, dv/v
```

The layout is deliberately `[time, variable, ...]` — the shape of an earth2studio `DataSource`
(`__call__(time, variable) -> DataArray`). A staged store is therefore one thin adapter away from being
a drop-in source inside an earth2studio pipeline, so the same tooling that drives the AI weather models
can drive the twin's forcing, hindcast, and observation streams. The staging layer *is* the twin's data
plane; earth2studio compatibility keeps it usable from the AI-weather ecosystem rather than siloed
([Appendix A](A-compute-and-deployment.qmd) has the compute and object-store detail).

## Assimilate and evaluate on the same footing

Assimilation reads the staged stores and applies the BLUE at each datum's native support
([Assimilation](04-assimilation.qmd)); the product is the 90 m GWL / soil-moisture / Vs30 fields with a
per-cell $\sigma$. The independent evaluation streams — ERA5-Land, GRACE, held-out SNOTEL/USCRN, the
gauges — pass through the *identical* fetch → homogenise → stage path, but are withheld from the update:
they **score** the product at their native resolution ([Evaluation data](02-evaluation-data.qmd)), they
never force it. Same plumbing, opposite role.
